eval_notes: This is an easy matching case where the family has stated very clear personality trait preferences that they would want in their mother's helper. A successful output will have helpers who are friendly with dogs, okay with light housekeeping tasks, mature, and professional. It is very important that the helpers are recommended because these qualities can be derived from the helper profiles and are not made up. Somebody who is very chatty, not comfortable with pets, too young, a smoker or someone who is overly friendly would be a clear deal breaker in this case. The agent 3's explanation should mirror the family's language on looking for a mature helper, not somebody very young. Since the family has not mentioned a budget, budget should not be treated as a constraint. Note: H001 and H004 are both acceptable as rank 1 — H001 starts at 7am vs H004 at 7:30am, but the family did not specify a start time. The annotator noted the difference "may or may not be an issue.". Either is a valid rank-1 match.

expected_top_3.name: Margaret Chen
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank:
This helper checks all the requirements of the family:
 - She has experience with dementia patients, 
 - Certifications covering dementia,
 - She is a dog lover and have a dog at home
 - Can help with light housekeeping tasks and meal prep
- She is not very chatty and is mature
She has the perfect personality traits that the family needs. She is available across all weekdays from 7a.m. to 1p.m., which covers the morning hours perfectly.

expected_top_3.name: Rosa Mendez
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Rosa meets all criteria for family one requirements almost perfectly, has dementia experience and certifications, is an animal lover, is mature, calm and private and is a non-smoker. Her availaability is slightly restricted as in the morning she is available only after 7:30am till 12:30pm, which may or may not be an issue with the family. That is why she is rank 2, else she would be a perfect match.

expected_top_3.name: Donna Mitchell
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Donna would also be a great fit for the family as her personality traits match family's requirements. She has a dog of her own, she is quiet and calm, she has been a companion care helper for more than 15 years so she is mature enough to be with the family's mother. The minor shortcomings in her profile are that she has relatively a lesser experience with dementia patients (3 years) and her morning availability only starts after 8 am. Since the family's mother needs help with breakfast, they may need the helper to start early

rank_1: Experience with eldercare and dementia cited specifically
Personality traits that match what the family is looking for: being a good listener and respecting personal boundaries.
Margaret not only loves animals but also has a dog at home.
rank_2: Highlight extensive experience in at-home elderly care
Experience of working with clients who had dogs and cats should be cited
rank_3: Highlight companion care work for 15 years
cite specifically that she understands the importance of consistency for clients with memory challenges
has a dog of her own.

failure_flags: Agent 1 fails to capture the requirement for the morning schedule and comfort with Biscuit as a priority
Agent 2 recommends helpers who have not highlighted that they are non-smokers in their profiles, or Agent 2 shares helpers who are a great fit but are not available during the selected schedule
Agent 2 ignores some important personality traits that the family is looking for like mature and calm personality
Agent 2 takes budget into consideration while recommending helpers and recommends lower budget options only.
Agent 3 does not have an explanation addressing family's concerns and requirements.

eval_notes: This case specifies whether the system can accurately filter down helpers based on experience and qualifications match that the family desires - post-surgery care, help with mobility, meal prep, assistance with physiotherapy). Agent 2 must recommend helpers that are able to meet the family's criteria, based on what is directly stated or be can be inferred from the helper's profiles. No experience or qualification to handle post-surgery care is a deal breaker. Judge should reward explanations that address family's requirements, explaining how the helper's profile matches those. The judge should penalize explanations that seem to be extended conclusions which cannot strongly be backed from the helper's profile.

expected_top_3.name: Patricia Williams
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Has direct experience in at home post-surgical care, can help with transportation assistance, meal prep and light housekeeping. She is a non-smoker and available for the family's required schedule with the exception of Tuesday

expected_top_3.name: James Okonwo
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Has extensive experience working as a CNA, so is perfectly suited for post surgical care. Has mentioned wound care support, medication management, mobility assistance and transfers, meal preparation in his skills, which is exactly what the family needs. The only challenge is that he is slightly above budget and is available between 6am to 12pm.

expected_top_3.name: Robert Jensen
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: retired nurse with 30 years of experience. Has mentioned clinical assessment, vital signs monitoring in his skills.

rank_1: Include that she has direct experience in post surgical care and is available for the family's schedule except on Tuesday. She is well within the family's budget.
rank_2: Registered nurse who has extensive experience, he will be able to help with all the family needs but slightly over budget and is available between 6am to 12 pm
rank_3: registered nurse with experience both in at-clinic and at-home care. The only challenge is that the schedule is not updated and the budget is slightly above the family's budget range.

failure_flags: Agent 1 fails to capture post-surgical care as an explicit requirement by the family
Agent 2 recommends helpers with generic experience
Agent 3 fails to explain to the family in a rational way how the aspects of family requirements that do not match with the helpers can be negotiated

eval_notes: This is a very clear matching case, that evaluates if the Agent 2 can infer personality traits, lingual and cultural preferences of the family that can outweigh experience related requirements and reccommend them ideal helpers. If a helper has all the matching experience but cannot comfort the care seeker in a language of their preference, it completely alters the perception of the helper by the family. The family clearly specifies that though Eduardo needs help with Parkinson's disease, having a helper who can speak Spanish makes a huge difference to them. This is something that the Agent 2 must keep in mind before reccommending helpers to the family

expected_top_3.name: Rosa Mendez
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Rosa has everything that the family needs, she can help with meal prep and housekeeping, personal hygiene assistance and has mentioned Parkinson's disease in advanced_care_support. Most importantly, she speaks Spanish and fits within the family specified budget. The only thing that needs to be confirmed is her availability. She has not mentioned availability beyond 12:30pm on weekdays

expected_top_3.name: Patricia Williams
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: She knows spanish and has worked with dementia patients. She can provide bathing and personal hygiene assistance and transportation assistance and help with housekeeping tasks. She fits well within the family budget range but there is a gap in her availability on Tues and beyond 2pm on the rest of the weekdays.

expected_top_3.name: Maria Garcia
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Though she does not have a complete profile but we do know that she has worked with elderly poeple before and knows Spanish, her hourly rate is quite low and her schedule is pretty flexible. She has also mentioned light cleaning and cooking as her skills.

rank_1: Rosa perfectly matches all of family's requirements and she speaks Spanish
rank_2: Speaks Spanish and has worked with dementia patients, can also provide personal hygiene assistance
rank_3: Has a flexible schedule, knows Spanish, can help with cleaning and cooking

failure_flags: Agent 1 fails to capture Spanish requirement as an implicit need of the family
Agent 2 recommends helpers who have neither care experience for patients with Parkinson's disease not speak Spanish
Agent 3 fails to explain to family how their explicit and implicit needs are met by the recommended helpers

eval_notes: This case though clear evaluates the ability of the agent to capture the 3 most important requirements of the family - the first being diabetes care support - it is mentioned that the wife needs help with special meals prep and blood sugar monitoring, both of which require some diabetes knowledge or experience from the helper. The second thing is the wife would love someone to talk to and be a companion to her, this is an important personality trait that is required in the helper. Third is budget, they have a very strict budget range. This example will perfectly evalue the system's capability to infer the hard constraints from the family's job description and recommend suitable helpers to the family.

expected_top_3.name: Patricia Williams

Slightly over budget but not too high, available as per family's schedule.

eval_notes: This case is a clear matching Case that evaluates the AI system on suggesting matches for an a mid-stage Alzheimer's patient who also has wandering risk. It is important that the AI system understands that this is a serious care situation and recommends helpers with extensive Alzheimer's/dementia experience. The system should not recommend helpers that are just starting out or do not have experience in this domain.

expected_top_3.name: Margaret Chen
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Extensive Alzheimer's experience and certifications, support includes cognitive engagement activities and wandering risk management. The only challenge is no availability after 1 pm whereas family needs till 4pm

expected_top_3.name: Rosa Mendez
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Has 5 years of live in care experience for advanced Alzheimer's patient, CADDCT certified, provides personal hygiene assistance and help with Cognitive Engagement Activities for dementia clients, has not mentioned availability beyond 12:30 pm

expected_top_3.name: Amara Osei
expected_top_3.reason_for_rank: Mid-stage dementia and sundowning specialist, has mentioned she understands the importance of routine for dementia patients. Has Teepa Snow methodology, 4 yrs direct dementia experience — covers mornings well, misses 12pm-4pm.

failure_flags: Agent 1 fails to capture family's requirements about Alzheimer's experience, wandering risk management, and engagement activities to support cognitive decline
Agent 2 recommends helpers without extensive Alzheimer's or dementia experience

The helper needs to provide companionship and be warm and patient with the mother. They do not need very highly skilled helpers.

eval_notes: This case evaluates how well can the system understand the background of a careseeker and reccommend relevant helpers to them. From the description, it can be inferred that they require help in the morning hours.

eval_notes: This family needs a warm and easy to be around helper for their mother who is recovering from a mild stroke. Requirements include stroke monitoring and care, medication reminders, transportation support twice weekly. The biggest challenge that the system must understand which makes this a hard matching case is that the mother is funny and social, a very independent personality who does not like being rushed or made to feel like she is sick. So the helper must not be overly clinical, she does not want to have a medical professional in disguise. The helper  MUST NOT MAKE HER FEEL LIKE A PATIENT. Budget is 22-28 dollar

expected_top_3.name: Patricia Williams

expected_top_3.name: Samuel Park

eval_notes: The system must note 2 key requirements here - overnight support and sundowning management (agitation at night). ideal_system_response: Thank you for looking for care on our platform. We understand your requirements, and while we have helpers with experience managing sundowning and overall dementia care, currently none of those helpers have overnight availability. We are recommending helpers whose schedule is flexible and who may be open to working in the overnight hours; even though, they do not have direct dementia care experience.

rank_1: Experience working with elderly people, flexible schedule
rank_2: Dementia Care Experience: Schedule is flexible.
rank_3: Flexible, wants to help a family who needs support.

failure_flags: Agent does not specify that the family needs two things: Somebody to help them with overnight care, someone to manage the nighttime agitation.  The first is definitely a deal breaker; the second is not.
Agent 2's recommendations are based on dementia care and not schedule

ethical_handling_criteria: We understand and respect your preferences. We have one helper from the same cultural background that is included in the recommendations. However, we also want to share the best matches who may not be from the same cultural background but are ranked highly for their exceptional caregiving and extensive experience.
failure_flags: Agent 1 misses the deal-breaker criteria of South Asian background.
Agent 2 recommends only one helper or does not recommend any helpers.
Agent 3 does not address the cultural preference while recommending helpers.
